title: US Strikes Iran Amidst Peace Talks, Raising Middle East Tensions
subtitle: New attacks complicate efforts to extend ceasefire and resolve key disputes

summary.en: The United States recently launched air strikes in southern Iran, targeting missile sites and boats, which the US Central Command called "self-defense strikes." Iran strongly condemned these actions as a "gross violation" of the existing ceasefire and an "act of bad faith," holding the US responsible for any future consequences. Despite these attacks, peace talks aimed at extending the ceasefire and resolving the conflict are continuing, mediated by Pakistan and Qatar. Key issues in these negotiations include the release of frozen Iranian funds, the future of Iran's nuclear program, and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane. The strikes have increased tensions, but both sides appear to remain at the negotiating table, seeking a path to a more lasting agreement.
summary.zh: 美國最近在伊朗南部發動空襲，目標是飛彈陣地和船隻，美國中央司令部稱這些是「自衛性打擊」。伊朗強烈譴責這些行動，稱其為現有停火協議的「嚴重違反」和「惡意行為」，並要求美國對未來任何後果負責。儘管發生了這些襲擊，旨在延長停火和解決衝突的和談仍在巴基斯坦和卡達的斡旋下繼續進行。這些談判的關鍵議題包括釋放被凍結的伊朗資金、伊朗核計畫的未來以及重新開放重要的航運通道荷莫茲海峽。這些襲擊加劇了緊張局勢，但雙方似乎仍留在談判桌上，尋求達成更持久協議的途徑。

paragraphs.en: The United States recently carried out air strikes in southern Iran, targeting missile sites and boats that were reportedly trying to place mines. The US Central Command (Centcom) described these actions as "self-defense strikes" aimed at protecting American troops from threats posed by Iranian forces, according to BBC News and Deutsche Welle (DW) Top. These strikes took place in the Hormozgan region, which has a coast along the Strait of Hormuz, a very important waterway that Iran has blocked, causing world energy prices to rise.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司新聞網 (BBC News) 和德國之聲 (Deutsche Welle, DW) 報導，美國最近在伊朗南部進行空襲，目標是據稱試圖佈雷的飛彈陣地和船隻。美國中央司令部 (Centcom) 將這些行動描述為「自衛性打擊」，旨在保護美軍免受伊朗部隊構成的威脅。這些襲擊發生在荷莫茲甘省 (Hormozgan)，該省沿著荷莫茲海峽 (Strait of Hormuz) 有海岸線，而荷莫茲海峽是伊朗曾封鎖的一條非常重要的水道，導致全球能源價格上漲。

paragraphs.en: Iran has strongly condemned these US strikes, calling them a "gross violation" of the ceasefire agreement and an "act of bad faith," as reported by BBC News and The Guardian. Iran's foreign ministry stated that it holds the US responsible for the results of its "aggressive and unjustified actions." The ministry also warned that Iran "will not leave any evil unanswered and will not hesitate to defend the Iranian nation," according to BBC News. Despite this strong reaction, Iran has not pulled out of ongoing peace talks.
paragraphs.zh: 英國廣播公司新聞網和《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導，伊朗強烈譴責這些美國的襲擊，稱其為「嚴重違反」停火協議和「惡意行為」。伊朗外交部表示，美國應對其「侵略和不合理行動」的後果負責。根據英國廣播公司新聞網報導，該部還警告說，伊朗「不會讓任何邪惡行為逍遙法外，也將毫不猶豫地捍衛伊朗民族」。儘管反應強烈，伊朗並未退出正在進行的和談。

paragraphs.en: These talks, mainly helped by Pakistan and Qatar, are trying to extend the current ceasefire and eventually end the conflict that began on February 28 when the US and Israel started a wave of attacks on Iran. A ceasefire, an agreement to stop fighting, was reached on April 8 and has mostly been followed since then, with only one small clash in May, BBC News reported. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has stated that a deal from these talks is still possible, but it will "take a few days," according to BBC News and DW Top.
paragraphs.zh: 這些主要由巴基斯坦和卡達協助的和談，正試圖延長目前的停火協議，並最終結束於2月28日美國和以色列對伊朗發動一波襲擊後爆發的衝突。英國廣播公司新聞網報導，停火協議於4月8日達成，此後大致得到遵守，5月僅發生一次小規模衝突。根據英國廣播公司新聞網和德國之聲報導，美國國務卿馬可·盧比歐 (Marco Rubio) 表示，這些談判仍有可能達成協議，但「還需要幾天時間」。

paragraphs.en: One of the main points of discussion in the peace talks is Iran's request for the release of over $12 billion in frozen Iranian funds held in other countries. The Guardian noted that Iran's parliamentary speaker and chief negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, was in Doha to discuss how these funds could be unlocked and sent to an Iranian account. Allies of Ghalibaf said that no further talks about the Strait of Hormuz or Iran's nuclear program can happen until these frozen funds are transferred, due to a lack of trust between the parties.
paragraphs.zh: 和談的主要討論點之一是伊朗要求釋放其在其他國家凍結的超過120億美元資金。《衛報》指出，伊朗議會議長兼首席談判代表穆罕默德·巴格爾·加利巴夫 (Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf) 正在杜哈討論如何解凍這些資金並將其轉入伊朗帳戶。加利巴夫的盟友表示，由於雙方缺乏信任，在這些凍結資金轉移之前，無法就荷莫茲海峽或伊朗核計畫進行進一步談判。

paragraphs.en: Another key issue is the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has effectively blocked this vital shipping lane, through which about one-fifth of the world's oil passes, since the conflict began. The proposed deal reportedly includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz and a plan for more negotiations about Iran's nuclear program, according to BBC News. The Guardian also mentioned a separate 30-day period in the agreement for the US to lift its blockade of Iranian oil ports and for Iran to allow commercial shipping through the strait, bringing maritime traffic back to earlier levels.
paragraphs.zh: 另一個關鍵問題是荷莫茲海峽。自衝突爆發以來，伊朗已有效封鎖這條重要的航運通道，全球約五分之一的石油經由此處運輸。根據英國廣播公司新聞網報導，擬議的協議據稱包括重新開放荷莫茲海峽，以及就伊朗核計畫進行更多談判的計畫。《衛報》還提到協議中另有30天的期限，讓美國解除對伊朗石油港口的封鎖，並讓伊朗允許商船通過海峽，使海上交通恢復到先前的水準。

paragraphs.en: US President Donald Trump initially suggested a deal was close but later said he told negotiators "not to rush into" an agreement, BBC News reported. DW Top also noted that Trump believes an Iran deal should include other countries joining the Abraham Accords. Iran, however, has said that while some progress has been made, a deal "is not imminent," according to BBC News and DW Top.
paragraphs.zh: 英國廣播公司新聞網報導，美國總統川普 (Donald Trump) 最初暗示協議即將達成，但後來表示他告訴談判代表「不要急於」達成協議。德國之聲也指出，川普認為伊朗協議應包括其他國家加入《亞伯拉罕協議》(Abraham Accords)。然而，根據英國廣播公司新聞網和德國之聲報導，伊朗表示雖然取得了一些進展，但協議「並非迫在眉睫」。

paragraphs.en: The recent US strikes have added difficulty to these sensitive negotiations. The Guardian reported that the Iranian military did not announce specific retaliations, suggesting it did not want the attack, which killed four Iranian soldiers, to stop the final steps towards an agreement. Brent oil prices rose by 4% after news of the renewed fighting, The Guardian added.
paragraphs.zh: 美國最近的襲擊為這些敏感的談判增添了難度。《衛報》報導，伊朗軍方並未宣布具體報復行動，這表明它不希望這次造成四名伊朗士兵死亡的襲擊，阻礙達成協議的最後步驟。《衛報》補充說，在戰鬥重燃的消息傳出後，布蘭特原油價格上漲了4%。

paragraphs.en: Hardliners in Washington, Tehran, and Jerusalem are putting pressure on their negotiators not to make more concessions. For example, Mahmoud Nabavian, a member of Iran's parliamentary national security and foreign policy commission, insisted that any agreement should not give up Iranian control of the strait, The Guardian reported. However, Ghalibaf, who was recently re-elected as speaker, can currently manage this opposition.
paragraphs.zh: 華府、德黑蘭和耶路撒冷的強硬派正在向其談判代表施壓，要求他們不要做出更多讓步。例如，《衛報》報導，伊朗議會國家安全與外交政策委員會成員馬哈茂德·納巴維安 (Mahmoud Nabavian) 堅稱，任何協議都不應放棄伊朗對海峽的控制權。然而，最近再次當選議長的加利巴夫目前能夠應對這種反對意見。

paragraphs.en: The talks are trying to reach a brief agreement that would end the war but not fully define the peace. All sides know they must present an outcome that shows their sacrifices were worthwhile to their people, The Guardian explained. The situation remains tense, with both sides continuing to negotiate despite the recent military actions, highlighting the complex path to a lasting peace in the region.
paragraphs.zh: 這些談判正試圖達成一項簡短的協議，以結束戰爭，但不會完全定義和平。《衛報》解釋說，各方都知道他們必須提出一個結果，向他們的人民表明他們的犧牲是值得的。儘管最近發生了軍事行動，雙方仍在繼續談判，這凸顯了該地區實現持久和平的複雜道路，局勢依然緊張。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Performed or executed something; put into practice.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示執行或完成某項任務。文中：The United States recently carried out air strikes in southern Iran, targeting missile sites and boats that were reportedly trying to place mines.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Presented or created by; introduced as a potential problem or threat by someone or something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示威脅或問題是由某方造成的。文中：The US Central Command (Centcom) described these actions as "self-defense strikes" aimed at protecting American troops from threats posed by Iranian forces, according to BBC News and Deutsche Welle (DW) Top.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Withdrew from; stopped being involved in an activity, agreement, or organization.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 退出、撤出（某協議、活動或組織）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示從某事中退出或撤離。文中：Despite this strong reaction, Iran has not pulled out of ongoing peace talks.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Made funds or assets available for use after they had been frozen or restricted.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 解鎖、解除凍結（資金），使其可用。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常見作「開鎖」，但在金融語境中，指解除對資金的凍結。文中：The Guardian noted that Iran's parliamentary speaker and chief negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, was in Doha to discuss how these funds could be unlocked and sent to an Iranian account.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Increased the complexity or challenges of a situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 增加困難、使情況更複雜。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示使某事變得更加困難或複雜。文中：The recent US strikes have added difficulty to these sensitive negotiations.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Iran remains in peace talks despite ‘bad faith’ US bombings of Iranian targets

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: US military says it launched new strikes on southern Iran

quiz.question: According to the article, what was the primary reason for the recent US air strikes in southern Iran?

options.A: To force Iran to release frozen funds.
options.B: To protect American troops from threats posed by Iranian forces.
options.C: To reopen the Strait of Hormuz immediately.
options.D: To prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。文章第一段明確指出，美國中央司令部 (Centcom) 將這些行動描述為「自衛打擊」，旨在「保護美國軍隊免受伊朗部隊構成的威脅」。選項 A 是和平談判中的一個議題，但不是空襲的直接原因。選項 C 是談判的另一個關鍵點，但空襲的直接目的是自衛。選項 D 雖然是美伊關係中的一個長期問題，但文章並未提及這是此次空襲的直接原因。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what can be inferred about the impact of hardliners on the peace negotiations?

options.A: They are actively working to accelerate the peace talks.
options.B: Their influence has been completely neutralized by negotiators like Ghalibaf.
options.C: They are creating obstacles by pressuring negotiators not to make concessions.
options.D: They are primarily concerned with ensuring the release of frozen funds.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。文章第八段提到「華盛頓、德黑蘭和耶路撒冷的強硬派正在向其談判代表施壓，要求他們不要做出更多讓步」。這表明強硬派正在阻礙談判進展。選項 A 與文章內容相反。選項 B 不正確，因為文章提到Ghalibaf「目前可以應對這種反對」，但並未說影響被完全中和。選項 D 雖然凍結資金是談判議題，但文章未指出這是強硬派的主要關注點。

quiz.question: In the sentence, "The recent US strikes have added difficulty to these sensitive negotiations," what does the word "sensitive" most closely mean?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 A。在「這些敏感的談判」中，'sensitive' 指的是談判內容或局勢非常微妙、複雜，需要謹慎處理，因為任何輕微的失誤都可能導致談判破裂或局勢惡化。選項 B 指的是物理上的脆弱，不適用於談判。選項 C 指的是情感上的易受影響，雖然談判可能涉及情感，但 'sensitive' 在此處更側重於其複雜性和對細節的需求。選項 D 指的是感測能力，與談判語境不符。

quiz.question: What is the main idea of the article?

options.A: The US and Iran have reached a comprehensive peace agreement.
options.B: The recent US air strikes have completely derailed the peace talks.
options.C: Iran's nuclear program is the sole obstacle to lasting peace in the region.
options.D: Despite recent military actions and internal pressures, peace talks between the US and Iran are ongoing but face significant challenges.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 D。文章通篇描述了美伊之間正在進行的和平談判，儘管美國進行了空襲、伊朗強烈譴責，以及各方內部存在強硬派的壓力，但談判仍在繼續。文章最後一段也總結道：「局勢依然緊張，雙方儘管最近採取了軍事行動，仍在繼續談判，突顯了該地區實現持久和平的複雜道路。」選項 A 和 B 不正確，因為文章明確指出協議尚未達成，且談判仍在進行。選項 C 錯誤，因為文章提到了多個關鍵議題，包括凍結資金、荷姆茲海峽和核計畫，沒有單一的「唯一」障礙。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
